message.content: Let's start migrating the Account object from Penguin to Stratus. There are about 2.4M rows. Should we do cursor-based or offset-based pagination?

content.text: For 2.4M rows with concurrent writes from the integration user, cursor-based pagination is strongly preferred over offset. Offset can produce inconsistent results if rows are inserted or deleted mid-pagination — we'd skip or double-process records. Cursor pagination using the Account Id gives deterministic ordering. I'll start with the migration script using Id-based cursors.

message.content: Yes, go ahead. Make sure we preserve Source_Id__c on Stratus.

content.text: The serializer already has Source_Id__c declared. I'll make sure the migration explicitly maps it and doesn't drop it. Downstream objects (Contact, Opportunity, AccountTeamMember) all use it for lookup resolution — removing it would break those migrations. Writing the migration now.

message.content: The migration is looping. It just keeps writing to Account_History__c. What's going on?

content.text:
That's a recursive trigger on Account_History__c firing. The trigger writes to Account_History__c on every Account insert/update — and since the migration is inserting Accounts, the history writes themselves are creating more triggered work. Feedback loop.

Fix: disable the Account_History__c trigger before the migration run, re-enable after. SOQL:

```sql
-- Before migration
UPDATE TriggerHandler__c SET Active__c = false WHERE Object__c = 'Account_History__c';
-- Run migration
-- After migration
UPDATE TriggerHandler__c SET Active__c = true WHERE Object__c = 'Account_History__c';
```

We should expect the same recursive-trigger pattern on Contact_History__c and Opportunity_History__c when those migrations run. I'll add this to scratch-notes.md so it's not lost.

message.content: Validation rule Account_Required_Industry is failing for about 30% of legacy records — they have null industry. What should we do?

content.text:
Two options:
1. Backfill Industry on the source (Penguin) side before migrating.
2. Disable the VR during migration and backfill on Stratus after.

Given 30% of records affected, option 2 is faster — backfilling on the source means a separate data-cleanup pass before we can even start migrating. Disabling the VR during migration lets us proceed now and clean up in parallel.

Disabling Account_Required_Industry now. Adding to the TODO list: backfill Industry post-migration and re-enable the VR.

message.content: One more thing — what's our rollback plan if this goes sideways? Specifically for AccountTeamMember.

content.text:
Critical: do NOT mass-delete AccountTeamMember rows on rollback. The delete cascades through user permission grants — once it cascades, restoring those permissions requires manual re-grants from an admin, and we lose audit trail.

Safer pattern: soft-delete. Set Active__c = false on the AccountTeamMember rows we want to roll back. We can flip them back to true if we need to restore. This is the pattern I'll use throughout the migration for any reversible state.

message.content: Migration completed for first 500k. Looks clean. One thing — should rate limits on /api/accounts use the global bucket or its own?

content.text: Leaning toward its own bucket because /api/accounts is high-volume and would dominate the global bucket — that would starve other endpoints. But this choice affects the dependent endpoints too (/api/contacts, /api/opportunities) since they'll likely follow the same pattern. Worth a quick sync with Om before committing — he's picking up Contact next, and the rate-limit decision is going to cascade into his work. Flagging as an open question in CLAUDE.md.
